records.ety: [OF. covrir, F. couvrir, fr. L. cooperire; co- + operire to cover; probably fr. ob towards, over + the root appearing in aperire to open. Cf. Aperient, Overt, Curfew.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Covered (-?rd); p. pr. & vb. n. Covering.]

defs: To overspread the surface of (one thing) with another; as, to cover wood with paint or lacquer; to cover a table with a cloth.
To envelop; to clothe, as with a mantle or cloak.
quotes: And with the majesty of darkness round Covers his throne.
All that beauty than doth cover thee.

defs: To invest (one's self with something); to bring upon (one's self); as, he covered himself with glory.
quotes: The powers that covered themselves with everlasting infamy by the partition of Poland.

defs: To hide sight; to conceal; to cloak; as, the enemy were covered from our sight by the woods.
quotes: In vain shou striv'st to cover shame with shame.

defs: To brood or sit on; to incubate.
quotes: While the hen is covering her eggs, the male . . . diverts her with his songs.
The waters returned and covered the chariots and the horsemen.

defs: To shelter, as from evil or danger; to protect; to defend; as, the cavalry covered the retreat.
quotes: His calm and blameless life Does with substantial blessedness abound, And the soft wings of peace cover him round.

defs: To remove from remembrance; to put away; to remit.

To extend over; to be sufficient for; to comprehend, include, or embrace; to account for or solve; to counterbalance; as, a mortgage which fully covers a sum loaned on it; a law which covers all possible cases of a crime; receipts than do not cover expenses.
To put the usual covering or headdress on.
quotes: Cover thy head . . . ; nay, prithee, be covered.

defs: To copulate with (a female); to serve; as, a horse covers a mare; -- said of the male.
Anything which is laid, set, or spread, upon, about, or over, another thing; an envelope; a lid; as, the cover of a book.
Anything which veils or conceals; a screen; disguise; a cloak.
Shelter; protection; as, the troops fought under cover of the batteries; the woods afforded a good cover.
quotes: Being compelled to lodge in the field . . . whilst his army was under cover, they might be forced to retire.

defs: The woods, underbrush, etc., which shelter and conceal game; covert; as, to beat a cover; to ride to cover.
The lap of a slide valve.
A tablecloth, and the other table furniture; esp., the table furniture for the use of one person at a meal; as, covers were laid for fifty guests.
quotes: Letters . . . dispatched under cover to her ladyship.

defs: To spread a table for a meal; to prepare a banquet.
